cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out the answer to this question about Babbage. The question is asking why he failed to continue his research work in 1828 mainly. The options are A through D. First, I'll go through the passage again to find the relevant part. The third paragraph mentions that when Babbage returned to London in 1828, he was at the end of his resources. Many bills were unpaid, his assistant and co-workers quarreled with him and left with expensive tools. For one year, no work was done. So the question is about the main reason he couldn't continue. The options are: A) poor health, B) ran out of money, C) co-workers argued, D) spent money on bills. Looking at the text, when he came back in 1828, the first thing mentioned is that he was at the end of his resources. Resources here likely refers to money. Then it says many bills remained unpaid. His co-workers quarreled and left, which is option C. But the question is the main reason. The passage says he was at the end of his resources (money) first, leading to unpaid bills and then the co-workers leaving. So the primary cause might be the lack of money (B). However, maybe the quarreling caused the work to stop? But the text says that after he returned, he had no resources, which led to unpaid bills, then co-workers left. So the chain of events starts with running out of money. Therefore, the main reason would be B. The co-workers leaving was a consequence of the financial problems.
